text:
Once upon a time, there was a bookworm named Oliver who lived in an ancient library. His favorite book? A timeless tale about a wise old owl named Owly. Every night, Oliver would fall asleep with Owly in his pocket, dreaming about endless forests and adventures.

One autumn, a terrible fog rolled in. So thick, you couldn’t see your own feet. Oliver stayed home, reading in his cozy nook. That’s when he heard it—the quiet crackle of something ancient.

Glancing out his window, he saw a bird with big, wise eyes. It chirped a melody so sweet that Oliver couldn’t pry himself away. The bird carried a scroll. When it landed gently on Oliver’s head, he opened it to find a series of ancient riddles.

“Who dreams of autumn leaves?” the riddle asked.

Oliver thought… “A poet.”

The next riddle spoke of a forest so deep, stars were jewels. “Who wanders it often
